examples: whoever is the closer has to turn out the lights and lock up

meanings.definition: (baseball) a relief pitcher who can protect a lead in the last inning or two of the game
at or within a short distance in space or time or having elements near each other
examples: how close are we to town?
we are all...in close sympathy with...

meanings.definition: not far distant in time or space or degree or circumstances
examples: a near hit by the bomb
kept a close watch on expenditures

meanings.definition: marked by fidelity to an original
examples: a faithful copy of the portrait
a faithful rendering of the observed facts

meanings.definition: (of a contest or contestants) evenly matched

examples: hot and stuffy and the air was blue with smoke
smooth percale with a very tight weave
very close (or near) with his money

meanings.definition: inclined to secrecy or reticence about divulging information
examples: although they knew her whereabouts her friends kept close about it

meanings.definition: (comparative of `near' or `close') within a shorter distance
examples: getting nearer to the true explanation
